# Check player position after RIGHT in vertical mode

# Find gray (g) nodes

for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'g':
        print(f"  Node {n['id']}: boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>

sections.content:
Interesting! After RIGHT in vertical mode:
- The player moved from rows 8-12, cols 14-18 to rows 8-12, cols 20-24
- The old position (rows 8-12, cols 14-18) became a black wall (BBBBB)
- The gray nodes at rows 8-12, cols 20-24 are gone (the player is now there)
So RIGHT DID move the player, even in vertical mode! The player moved from cols 14-18 to cols 20-24.
Wait, but earlier (step 10), RIGHT didn't move the player. Let me re-examine.
After step 9 (DOWN), the player was at rows 14-18, cols 14-18. After step 10 (RIGHT), the player was still at rows 14-18, cols 14-18. So RIGHT didn't move the player in that case.
But after step 11 (UP), the player was at rows 8-12, cols 14-18. After step 12 (RIGHT), the player moved to rows 8-12, cols 20-24. So RIGHT did move the player in this case.
The difference is that in step 10, the player was at rows 14-18, cols 14-18, and there was no chamber to the right (cols 20-24 was a white passage, not a blue chamber). In step 12, the player was at rows 8-12, cols 14-18, and there was a gray chamber to the right (cols 20-24).
So the player can only move to chambers (blue or gray), not to white passages or walls.
Wait, but the player moved to the gray chamber at cols 20-24. So the player can move to gray chambers.
Actually, looking at the current state, the player is at rows 8-12, cols 20-24 (blue). The old position (rows 8-12, cols 14-18) is a black wall. The gray nodes are gone (only the small gray node at rows 1-3, cols 1-3 remains, which is the HUD element).
So the mechanic is:
- The player can move to adjacent chambers (blue or gray).
- When the player leaves a chamber, it becomes a black wall.
- The player can move in both horizontal and vertical directions, but only to chambers.
But wait, earlier the player couldn't move RIGHT in vertical mode (step 10). Let me re-examine.
After step 9 (DOWN), the player was at rows 14-18, cols 14-18. To the right (cols 20-24) was a white passage (WWWWW), not a chamber. So the player couldn't move RIGHT because there was no chamber to the right.
After step 11 (UP), the player was at rows 8-12, cols 14-18. To the right (cols 20-24) was a gray chamber (ggggg). So the player could move RIGHT because there was a chamber to the right.
So the mechanic is:
- The player can move to adjacent chambers (blue or gray), regardless of the mode.
- The mode (horizontal/vertical) only affects which directions the player can move.
Wait, but after step 11 (UP), the player was in vertical mode, and RIGHT moved the player. So the mode doesn't restrict the direction.
Hmm, let me re-examine. After step 9 (DOWN), the player was in vertical mode. RIGHT didn't move the player because there was no chamber to the right. After step 11 (UP), the player was in vertical mode. RIGHT moved the player because there was a gray chamber to the right.
So the mode doesn't restrict the direction. The player can move in any direction, but only to adjacent chambers.
But wait, earlier the player couldn't move DOWN without pressing SPACE. Let me re-examine.
After step 3 (LEFT), the player was at rows 8-12, cols 14-18 (horizontal mode). The player couldn't move DOWN because there was no chamber below (rows 14-18, cols 14-18 was a black wall).
After step 4 (SPACE), the player was at rows 8-12, cols 14-18 (vertical mode). The player could move DOWN because there was a chamber below (rows 14-18, cols 14-18 became a blue chamber).
So SPACE doesn't just toggle the mode; it also changes the maze structure. After SPACE, new chambers appear in the vertical direction.
Let me re-examine the maze structure changes after SPACE:
- Before SPACE (step 4): Rows 14-18, cols 14-18 was a black wall.
- After SPACE (step 4): Rows 14-18, cols 14-18 became a blue chamber.
So SPACE opens up chambers in the vertical direction.
Similarly, after SPACE (step 8), the maze structure changed again. Let me check what changed.
Actually, I think the mechanic is:
- SPACE toggles between horizontal and vertical modes.
- In horizontal mode, chambers are available in the horizontal direction (LEFT/RIGHT).
- In vertical mode, chambers are available in the vertical direction (UP/DOWN).
- The player can only move to chambers that are available in the current mode.
But this doesn't explain why RIGHT moved the player in vertical mode (step 12).
Let me re-examine step 12. After step 11 (UP), the player was at rows 8-12, cols 14-18. The chamber at rows 8-12, cols 20-24 was gray (visited earlier). RIGHT moved the player to this gray chamber.
So the player can move to gray chambers regardless of the mode. Gray chambers are "visited" chambers, and the player can always move to them.
But the player can only move to blue chambers in the current mode's direction. In horizontal mode, the player can move to blue chambers in the horizontal direction. In vertical mode, the player can move to blue chambers in the vertical direction.
Hmm, this is getting complicated. Let me simplify.
The player is currently at rows 8-12, cols 20-24. Let me check what chambers are available:
- To the left: rows 8-12, cols 14-18 (black wall)
- To the right: rows 8-12, cols 25-39 (black wall)
- Below: rows 14-18, cols 20-24 (white passage)
- Above: rows 7, cols 20-24 (black wall)
So there are no adjacent chambers. The player is stuck.
Let me try SPACE to toggle the mode and see what chambers appear.
